_meta.note: Offline fallback mirror of the live nanopub-network verdict index (CiTO->DOI, retraction/supersession-filtered). Regenerate by snapshotting replication_radar.network.build_index(). Records now carry the targeted FORRT claim {label, aida, type}.

claim.label: Effect of HEALPix DGGS Indexing on Geospatial Data Performance
claim.aida: HEALPix indexing via healpix-geo achieves equivalent DGGS performance to H3 on both sphere and WGS84 ellipsoid.

claim.label: Effect of DGGS Indexing on Associating Vector and Raster Geospatial Data
claim.aida: Indexing geospatial data to a DGGS makes vector and raster data associable using zone IDs as join keys.

claim.label: Effect of DGGS Indexing on Associating Vector and Raster Geospatial Data
claim.aida: Indexing geospatial data to a DGGS makes vector and raster data associable using zone IDs as join keys.

claim.label: ZKP-based privacy-preserving environmental compliance verification
claim.aida: Zero-knowledge proofs based on zk-SNARKs can verify that environmental sensor readings satisfy regulatory compliance thresholds without revealing the underlying measurement values.

claim.label: SST as primary driver of fish community structure (NE Atlantic)
claim.aida: Sea surface temperature is the primary driver of fish community structure across Northeast Atlantic shelf seas.

claim.label: SST as primary driver of fish community structure (NE Atlantic)
claim.aida: Sea surface temperature is the primary driver of fish community structure across Northeast Atlantic shelf seas.

claim.label: SST as primary driver of fish community structure (NE Atlantic)
claim.aida: Sea surface temperature is the primary driver of fish community structure across Northeast Atlantic shelf seas.

claim.label: Cross-domain few-shot benchmark results depend on exact architecture and training setup
claim.aida: Cross-domain few-shot classification accuracy on Sentinel-2 satellite imagery depends on matching the backbone architecture, image resolution, and training procedure of the original benchmark.

claim.label: Cross-domain few-shot benchmark results depend on exact architecture and training setup
claim.aida: Cross-domain few-shot classification accuracy on Sentinel-2 satellite imagery depends on matching the backbone architecture, image resolution, and training procedure of the original benchmark.

claim.label: Supervised pretraining matches episodic meta-learning for cross-domain few-shot satellite classification
claim.aida: Standard supervised pretraining achieves comparable cross-domain few-shot classification accuracy on satellite imagery to episodic meta-learning with less training time.

claim.label: Prototypical Networks transfer from photographs to satellite imagery with a simple backbone
claim.aida: Prototypical Networks classify land cover types in Sentinel-2 satellite imagery from a small number of labeled examples.

claim.label: HEALPix DGGS conversion of EarthCARE Level-2 preserves variable-type aggregation semantics
claim.aida: EarthCARE Level-2 data from the Multi-Spectral Imager (MSI), Atmospheric Lidar (ATLID), and Cloud Profiling Radar (CPR) can be converted to HEALPix Discrete Global Grid System cells on the WGS84 ellipsoid while preserving variable-type aggregation semantics.

claim.label: Increasing thermal exposure raises local extirpation rate in bumble bee species
claim.aida: Local extirpation rate in bumble bee species increases with the frequency at which local temperatures exceed species-specific historically observed thermal tolerances.

claim.label: Increasing thermal exposure raises local extirpation rate in bumble bee species
claim.aida: Local extirpation rate in bumble bee species increases with the frequency at which local temperatures exceed species-specific historically observed thermal tolerances.

claim.label: TEI_delta GLMM coefficient is positive and credibly above zero on Iberian Bombus at HEALPix nside=64
claim.aida: Increased thermal exposure between historical baseline and recent climate predicts higher probability of local extirpation in Iberian Bombus populations.

claim.label: TEI_delta GLMM coefficient is positive and credibly above zero on Iberian Bombus at HEALPix nside=64
claim.aida: Increased thermal exposure between historical baseline and recent climate predicts higher probability of local extirpation in Iberian Bombus populations.

claim.label: TEI-based extirpation projection per-species ranking is grid-coupled at the projection step for low-N species
claim.aida: Projected per-species rankings of Iberian Bombus extirpation risk under future climate are sensitive to the spatial grid resolution of the analysis, particularly for species with sparse historical distribution.

claim.label: Phytoplankton CNN classification across 95 classes
claim.aida: A convolutional neural network identifies phytoplankton taxa across 95 classes with 86.3% top-1 accuracy and 98.8% top-5 accuracy.

claim.label: Scattering features complement CNN for plankton classification
claim.aida: Stacking multi-scale scattering features on top of a convolutional neural network's softmax probabilities lifts mean rare-class recall on plankton classification by 8.4 percentage points at a 0.72 percentage-point cost to aggregate top-1 accuracy.

claim.label: Spherical scattering transform synthesis
claim.aida: Scattering transforms on the sphere can synthesise astrophysical maps that match the multi-scale non-Gaussian statistics of a target input.

claim.label: SST gap-filling via scattering transforms
claim.aida: Scattering transforms can fill cloud gaps in satellite sea surface temperature observations using a gap-free reference product as a statistical target.

claim.label: HEALPix family is fit for purpose as the common DGGS substrate for biodiversity × Copernicus EO × Destination Earth integration
claim.aida: The HEALPix family — including HEALPix-on-WGS84 via authalic-sphere mapping (healpix-geo / Ellipsoidal HEALPix) and rHEALPix — is fit for purpose as the common Discrete Global Grid System substrate for integrating biodiversity occurrence data with high-resolution Copernicus Earth-observation products and Destination Earth climate-model output.

claim.label: HEALPix family is fit for purpose as the common DGGS substrate for biodiversity × Copernicus EO × Destination Earth integration
claim.aida: The HEALPix family — including HEALPix-on-WGS84 via authalic-sphere mapping (healpix-geo / Ellipsoidal HEALPix) and rHEALPix — is fit for purpose as the common Discrete Global Grid System substrate for integrating biodiversity occurrence data with high-resolution Copernicus Earth-observation products and Destination Earth climate-model output.

claim.label: HEALPix family is fit for purpose as the common DGGS substrate for biodiversity × Copernicus EO × Destination Earth integration
claim.aida: The HEALPix family — including HEALPix-on-WGS84 via authalic-sphere mapping (healpix-geo / Ellipsoidal HEALPix) and rHEALPix — is fit for purpose as the common Discrete Global Grid System substrate for integrating biodiversity occurrence data with high-resolution Copernicus Earth-observation products and Destination Earth climate-model output.

claim.label: HEALPix family is fit for purpose as the common DGGS substrate for biodiversity × Copernicus EO × Destination Earth integration
claim.aida: The HEALPix family — including HEALPix-on-WGS84 via authalic-sphere mapping (healpix-geo / Ellipsoidal HEALPix) and rHEALPix — is fit for purpose as the common Discrete Global Grid System substrate for integrating biodiversity occurrence data with high-resolution Copernicus Earth-observation products and Destination Earth climate-model output.

claim.label: Sphere-harmonic matched filter on HEALPix maintains detection accuracy at high latitudes; lat-lon flat matched filter does not
claim.aida: On the HEALPix-NESTED substrate, a sphere-harmonic band-pass matched filter trained on low-latitude marine-heatwave events maintains 1.000 detection accuracy at the 70-80° latitude band while an equivalent lat-lon flat matched filter trained identically collapses to 0.500 detection accuracy at the same band.

claim.label: Sphere-harmonic matched filter on HEALPix transfers cross-discipline at 1.000 without retraining; lat-lon flat matched filter drops to 0.845 on the same transfer
claim.aida: On the HEALPix-NESTED substrate, a sphere-harmonic band-pass matched filter trained on a cosmology-like Domain A with features at uniformly-random sphere locations classifies a climate-like Domain B with features confined to high latitudes at 1.000 accuracy without retraining, while an equivalent lat-lon flat matched filter trained identically drops from 1.000 in-domain to 0.845 on the same cross-domain transfer.

claim.label: Sphere-harmonic matched filter on HEALPix maintains detection accuracy at high latitudes; lat-lon flat matched filter does not
claim.aida: On the HEALPix-NESTED substrate, a sphere-harmonic band-pass matched filter trained on low-latitude marine-heatwave events maintains 1.000 detection accuracy at the 70-80° latitude band while an equivalent lat-lon flat matched filter trained identically collapses to 0.500 detection accuracy at the same band.

claim.label: Sphere-harmonic matched filter on HEALPix transfers cross-discipline at 1.000 without retraining; lat-lon flat matched filter drops to 0.845 on the same transfer
claim.aida: On the HEALPix-NESTED substrate, a sphere-harmonic band-pass matched filter trained on a cosmology-like Domain A with features at uniformly-random sphere locations classifies a climate-like Domain B with features confined to high latitudes at 1.000 accuracy without retraining, while an equivalent lat-lon flat matched filter trained identically drops from 1.000 in-domain to 0.845 on the same cross-domain transfer.

claim.label: 94 percent of marine GBIF records in the Western Australian region during 2011 sat on HEALPix cells that experienced MHW conditions during the documented Ningaloo Niño event
claim.aida: On the HEALPix-NESTED substrate, 719 of 765 (94.0 percent) marine biodiversity occurrences from GBIF in the Western Australian region during 2011 sat on cells that experienced marine-heatwave conditions during the documented Ningaloo Niño event of February-March 2011.

claim.label: Coastal-ROM trade-off in coupled ADCIRC+SWAN hindcasts (Loveland et al. 2024)
claim.aida: In coupled ADCIRC SWAN (storm-surge plus spectral-wave) hindcasts of Gulf of Mexico hurricanes, the third-generation ST6 source-term package in SWAN yields no improvement in water-surface-elevation root-mean-square error at NOAA gauges over the first- or second-generation source-term packages, while increasing wall-clock run time.

claim.label: Target-group background improves presence-only SDM AUC
claim.aida: Using target-group background increases the mean AUC of presence-only species distribution models relative to using random background.

claim.label: Few-shot Prototypical Networks generalize to novel satellite land cover classes
claim.aida: Prototypical Networks classify land cover types in Sentinel-2 satellite imagery from a small number of labeled examples.

claim.label: Sinervo 2010 h_r mechanism for heliothermic lizard local extinction
claim.aida: Heliothermic lizard populations experience local extinction when the cumulative daily hours of activity restriction during the critical reproductive period (h_r, computed as the hours per day when operative temperature exceeds the species' preferred body temperature) exceed a family-specific threshold.

claim.label: Scale-dependent misidentification of bird richness hotspots from range maps (Hurlbert & Jetz 2007)
claim.aida: Aggregating expert-drawn bird range maps onto spatial grids finer than approximately two degrees misidentifies up to two-thirds of species-richness hotspots relative to atlas survey data.

claim.label: Arctic Amplification ratio — Rantanen et al. 2022
claim.aida: The mean Arctic Amplification ratio (Arctic OLS warming trend / global OLS warming trend, 1979–2021) was 3.8 across four observational datasets (ERA5, GISTEMP v4, Berkeley Earth, HadCRUT5), indicating that the Arctic warmed approximately four times faster than the global mean.

claim.label: Open temperature-at-depth geolocation reproduces juvenile white shark positions less accurately than the original light-based method
claim.aida: In juvenile white sharks tracked by archival tags, an open hidden Markov geolocation method that matches tag temperature-at-depth against an ocean reanalysis reproduces the released satellite-tracked positions less accurately than the original proprietary light-based method.

claim.label: PAT light-level geolocation of juvenile white sharks
claim.aida: Pop-up archival tag light-level data yield accurate daily geolocations of juvenile white sharks in the northeast Pacific.
